feature_name: Hide hamburger menu when sidebar is visible on desktop

tasks.title: Hide .nav-toggle on desktop via CSS + add E2E smoke coverage
tasks.description: Add `display: none;` as the first declaration in the base `.nav-toggle` rule in `web/src/routes/+layout.svelte` (around line 726). The existing `@media (max-width: 900px)` block at ~line 867 already restores `display: inline-flex` for mobile, so no second edit is needed. Then add a new E2E spec at `web/tests/e2e/hamburger-desktop.spec.ts` tagged `@smoke` with two cases: (1) desktop viewport 1280x800 asserts `button[aria-label="Toggle navigation"]` is not visible; (2) mobile viewport 390x844 asserts the button is visible, clicking it opens the sidebar drawer.
tasks.done_when: (a) `.nav-toggle` base rule contains `display: none;` and the markup at lines 411-413 is unchanged. (b) `web/tests/e2e/hamburger-desktop.spec.ts` exists with both viewport cases, tagged `@smoke`. (c) `cd web && npm run lint`, `cd web && npm run check`, `cd web && npm run test`, and `cd web && npm run test:e2e:smoke` all pass locally. (d) Diff is +1 line in `+layout.svelte` plus the new test file; no other files touched.

history.note: Feature initiated in worktree /home/coder/projects/tasksync-hamburger on branch feat/hide-hamburger-on-desktop. Goal: hide hamburger button when the sidebar is permanently visible (desktop), reclaim its space so the TaskSync logo shifts left. Mobile/narrow viewport unchanged. Polish-only scope; no header/sidebar-toggle refactor.

Routed to PM. Three decisions are locked: (1) hide trigger = sidebar visibility (equivalent to desktop breakpoint), (2) reclaim space via {#if} or display:none, not visibility:hidden, (3) mobile/narrow viewport keeps hamburger. PM must not re-litigate these.

PM discovery complete. Hamburger located in web/src/routes/+layout.svelte line 411 (button.nav-toggle). Sidebar-visibility signal is the existing @media (max-width: 900px) CSS breakpoint — no new store needed. Exec plan written to docs/exec-plans/active/2026-05-10-hide-hamburger-on-desktop.md.

Routed to PE. Choice deferred from PM: CSS-only display:none at desktop breakpoint vs Svelte {#if} with matchMedia. Strong bias toward CSS-only (smaller diff, no JS, no SSR/hydration edge cases). PE to append Design section to existing exec plan.

PE design complete. Chosen approach: CSS-only — add `display: none;` to the base `.nav-toggle` rule (~line 726). Existing @media (max-width: 900px) block at ~line 867 already restores `display: inline-flex`, so no second edit. Architecture impact: none. Expected diff: +1 line in +layout.svelte plus a new E2E spec.

EM task breakdown: single combined task T1 — apply the +1 CSS line and add `web/tests/e2e/hamburger-desktop.spec.ts` with both viewport cases. Not over-decomposed because the CSS diff is trivial and the test directly validates it; splitting would just add ceremony.

Routed T1 to SDE. Inbox written to .state/inbox/software-developer.md with exact CSS snippet from PE design, E2E test structure (Playwright @smoke, 1280x800 desktop hidden + 375x667 mobile visible), and quality gates to run. Reinforced: do not touch the existing @media (max-width: 900px) block — it already restores display: inline-flex via cascade.

SDE shipped T1: +display:none; in .nav-toggle base rule of web/src/routes/+layout.svelte (line 727), plus new web/tests/e2e/hamburger-desktop.spec.ts with two @smoke cases (desktop 1280x800 hidden, mobile 390x844 visible+clickable). SDE ran lint/check/vitest/smoke locally — all pass. T1 marked done.
Routing to build-specialist for full quality gate sweep in worktree. Frontend-only change; cargo checks explicitly skipped.

Build-specialist results: lint pass, check pass, vitest 366/366 pass, Playwright @smoke 20/20 pass on Chromium (including the two new hamburger-desktop cases). All four frontend gates green. Cargo checks intentionally skipped — no Rust changes.
Routing to PM for acceptance validation against the four criteria in the exec plan. PM inspects diff conceptually (CSS cascade trace) and confirms pass/fail per criterion.

PM acceptance: 4/4 criteria PASS. Cascade trace confirmed (>900px: base rule display:none wins, no media-query override; ≤900px: media-query block restores display:inline-flex). Overall verdict: APPROVE.
Exec plan archived to docs/exec-plans/completed/. CLAUDE.md completed-plans list updated. Ready to commit, push, and open PR.
